title: Vernonanthura cocleana
intro: Vernonanthura cocleana, also known as Brazilian ironweed, is a plant species native to the Brazilian savannas, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Vernonanthura cocleana include flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Vernonanthura cocleana include teas, infusions, and extracts, often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Vernonanthura cocleana is a plant species that belongs to the family Asteraceae. This plant is a member of the Vernonanthura genus, which includes various species of flowering plants native to South America. Vernonanthura cocleana is often classified within the subfamily Vernonantheae. The classification of Vernonanthura cocleana is further supported by its characteristics, such as its leaves and flowering patterns. These features are used to distinguish Vernonanthura cocleana from other plant species within the same genus and family.
